notes: This file exposes the approved controller-volume breathing approximation variables already wired into the Sussex APK.
The companion should compile this JSON into runtime_overrides.csv and upload it through the normal Sussex hotload path.
Applying these controls resets controller-breathing calibration in the runtime. Recalibrate before a participant run.

intent: Tune the existing Sussex controller-volume breathing approximation without changing controller routing, active hand selection, or scene topology.

info.effect: Switches between principal-axis solve and live-axis warmup calibration.
info.increaseLooksLike: Enabled keeps the learned axis tied to dominant motion.
info.decreaseLooksLike: Disabled uses the live controller axis after warmup.
tradeoffs: Enabled is usually more robust.
Disabled can simplify recovery if the solved axis is unstable.

info.effect: Sets how long live-axis warmup runs before learning.
info.increaseLooksLike: Warmup spends longer stabilizing the axis.
info.decreaseLooksLike: Learning begins sooner.
tradeoffs: Higher values can steady the axis.
Lower values shorten setup.

info.effect: Sets the warmup frame count before learning starts.
info.increaseLooksLike: Warmup needs more valid frames.
info.decreaseLooksLike: Warmup finishes sooner.
tradeoffs: Higher values stabilize the axis.
Lower values reduce setup time.

info.effect: Sets the learning time budget during calibration.
info.increaseLooksLike: Calibration has longer to gather motion.
info.decreaseLooksLike: Calibration times out sooner.
tradeoffs: Higher values are more forgiving.
Lower values fail faster when motion is poor.

info.effect: Sets how many accepted frames are needed before solving bounds.
info.increaseLooksLike: Calibration demands a longer clean motion sample.
info.decreaseLooksLike: Calibration can finish with fewer samples.
tradeoffs: Higher values improve confidence.
Lower values reduce setup effort.

info.effect: Sets the total timeout before calibration gives up.
info.increaseLooksLike: Calibration waits longer before timing out.
info.decreaseLooksLike: Calibration fails faster.
tradeoffs: Higher values forgive slow starts.
Lower values surface failures sooner.

info.effect: Flips classifier polarity for left-hand runs.
info.increaseLooksLike: Enabled preserves the current left-hand inversion baseline.
info.decreaseLooksLike: Disabled keeps the same polarity for both hands.
tradeoffs: Keep this enabled unless left-hand labeling is clearly reversed.
Disabling it can invert left-hand inhale and exhale interpretation.

info.effect: Sets the positive threshold for inhale classification.
info.increaseLooksLike: Stronger motion is needed before inhale triggers.
info.decreaseLooksLike: Smaller positive motion can trigger inhale.
tradeoffs: Higher values reject small jitters.
Lower values feel more sensitive.

info.effect: Sets the negative threshold for exhale classification.
info.increaseLooksLike: Moving it toward zero makes exhale easier to trigger.
info.decreaseLooksLike: More negative values need stronger reverse motion.
tradeoffs: Values too close to zero can react to jitter.
Values too negative can delay exhale.

info.effect: Rejects classifier updates when wrist rotation is too large.
info.increaseLooksLike: More rotation is tolerated.
info.decreaseLooksLike: Rotation noise is guarded more strictly.
tradeoffs: Higher values allow freer wrist motion.
Lower values protect against rotation spikes.

info.effect: Guards classification when moving-average drift is too large.
info.increaseLooksLike: Slow drift is tolerated more easily.
info.decreaseLooksLike: Drift is guarded more strictly.
tradeoffs: Higher values reduce false guards on posture drift.
Lower values keep the classifier tighter.

info.effect: Sets the short classifier smoothing window.
info.increaseLooksLike: Short-term changes are smoothed more heavily.
info.decreaseLooksLike: The classifier reacts more quickly.
tradeoffs: Higher values reduce jitter.
Lower values improve responsiveness.

info.effect: Sets the long classifier smoothing window.
info.increaseLooksLike: Long-term context changes more slowly.
info.decreaseLooksLike: Long-term context adapts more quickly.
tradeoffs: Higher values stabilize the long reference.
Lower values respond faster to change.

info.effect: Sets the smallest motion change that still counts as a new accepted sample.
info.increaseLooksLike: Calibration ignores smaller changes and waits for larger motion.
info.decreaseLooksLike: Calibration accepts smaller changes as meaningful motion.
tradeoffs: Higher values reject micro-jitter more aggressively.
Lower values can accept more noise.

info.effect: Sets the travel span calibration must observe before it accepts bounds.
info.increaseLooksLike: Calibration demands a larger motion excursion.
info.decreaseLooksLike: Calibration can accept shorter motion ranges.
tradeoffs: Higher values avoid tiny low-quality calibrations.
Lower values can help very small-motion users.

info.effect: Turns adaptive bound updates on or off after calibration.
info.increaseLooksLike: Enabled lets the runtime keep adapting bounds over time.
info.decreaseLooksLike: Disabled freezes the calibrated bounds.
tradeoffs: Adaptive bounds can track slow drift.
Disabling adaptation keeps the mapping stable.

info.effect: Shrinks raw bounds inward before the final mapping is applied.
info.increaseLooksLike: The usable range becomes more conservative near the edges.
info.decreaseLooksLike: More of the raw observed span is preserved.
tradeoffs: Higher margins reduce edge surprises.
Lower margins use more of the observed span.

info.effect: Applies extra easing near the solved bounds.
info.increaseLooksLike: The extremes soften more before hitting the final limits.
info.decreaseLooksLike: The mapping stays more direct near the extremes.
tradeoffs: Higher values feel less abrupt at the edges.
Lower values preserve more raw range.

info.effect: Controls how aggressively adaptive bounds move toward new solved values.
info.increaseLooksLike: Bounds move more quickly when adaptation fires.
info.decreaseLooksLike: Bounds change more gradually.
tradeoffs: Higher values react faster to drift.
Lower values are smoother but slower.

info.effect: Prevents adaptive bounds from collapsing below a fraction of the original span.
info.increaseLooksLike: Adaptive bounds preserve more of the initial span.
info.decreaseLooksLike: Adaptive bounds can shrink more aggressively.
tradeoffs: Higher values keep the mapping broad.
Lower values let the mapping tighten.

info.effect: Sets the lower quantile used when solving bounds.
info.increaseLooksLike: The lower bound ignores more low-end outliers.
info.decreaseLooksLike: More of the low-end span is preserved.
tradeoffs: Higher values reject more low-end outliers.
Lower values keep more observed range.

info.effect: Sets the upper quantile used when solving bounds.
info.increaseLooksLike: More high-end motion is preserved before it counts as outlier.
info.decreaseLooksLike: The upper bound rejects more high-end extremes.
tradeoffs: Higher values preserve more of the top end.
Lower values make the upper bound more conservative.

info.effect: Sets the time window used for adaptive bound samples.
info.increaseLooksLike: Adaptation looks at a longer recent history.
info.decreaseLooksLike: Adaptation reacts to a shorter history.
tradeoffs: Higher values smooth longer-term behavior.
Lower values react more quickly.

info.effect: Sets the maximum accepted-sample count kept for adaptive bounds.
info.increaseLooksLike: Adaptation uses a larger accepted-sample buffer.
info.decreaseLooksLike: Adaptation keeps a smaller recent buffer.
tradeoffs: Higher values stabilize the adaptive solve.
Lower values make it more reactive.

info.effect: Sets the minimum time between adaptive-bound recalculations.
info.increaseLooksLike: Adaptive updates happen less often.
info.decreaseLooksLike: Adaptive updates can happen more often.
tradeoffs: Higher values reduce churn.
Lower values make the mapping more responsive.

info.effect: Sets how many fresh adaptive samples are needed before recalculation can happen.
info.increaseLooksLike: Adaptation waits for more new samples.
info.decreaseLooksLike: Bounds can update after fewer fresh samples.
tradeoffs: Higher values reduce jitter in bound updates.
Lower values react faster.

info.effect: Sets the minimum clean-frame ratio calibration requires before it accepts a solve.
info.increaseLooksLike: Calibration becomes stricter about bad or missing frames.
info.decreaseLooksLike: Calibration tolerates noisier input.
tradeoffs: Higher values protect quality.
Lower values can help in messy tracking.

info.effect: Controls how often the approximation emits analysis frames.
info.increaseLooksLike: The runtime samples controller motion more often.
info.decreaseLooksLike: The approximation updates on a slower cadence.
tradeoffs: Higher rates can improve responsiveness.
Lower rates reduce sensitivity to micro-motions.

info.effect: Rejects frames whose rotation jump is too large.
info.increaseLooksLike: Larger rotation jumps are still accepted.
info.decreaseLooksLike: The runtime is stricter about rotation spikes.
tradeoffs: Higher values allow freer wrist motion.
Lower values protect against rotation spikes.

info.effect: Rejects frames whose position jump is too large.
info.increaseLooksLike: Larger position jumps are still accepted.
info.decreaseLooksLike: The runtime is stricter about translation spikes.
tradeoffs: Higher values are more tolerant of abrupt motion.
Lower values protect against outlier jumps.

info.effect: Controls the exponential moving-average blend for the projection signal.
info.increaseLooksLike: The smoothed signal follows new motion more quickly.
info.decreaseLooksLike: The smoothed signal becomes steadier and slower.
tradeoffs: Higher values improve responsiveness.
Lower values suppress jitter more strongly.

info.effect: Sets the median filter window used before the EMA stage.
info.increaseLooksLike: More neighboring samples are considered before the EMA.
info.decreaseLooksLike: The filter uses a smaller neighborhood.
tradeoffs: Higher values reject isolated spikes better.
Lower values feel more immediate.

info.effect: Flattens a zone around the center of the normalized volume mapping.
info.increaseLooksLike: More of the middle range is held near the midpoint.
info.decreaseLooksLike: The mapping stays continuous through the midpoint.
tradeoffs: Higher values can steady the midpoint.
Lower values preserve continuous variation.

info.effect: Shapes the final normalized volume curve after bounds and deadband.
info.increaseLooksLike: Higher portions of the range are emphasized more strongly.
info.decreaseLooksLike: The mapping gives more weight to lower portions of the range.
tradeoffs: Higher gamma can delay visible rise until later in the motion.
Lower gamma can make early motion feel stronger.

crossFieldValidation: controls.lower_quantile.value must be less than or equal to controls.upper_quantile.value
controls.short_window.value must be less than or equal to controls.long_window.value

compilerHints.outputContract: Compile this self-describing file into runtime_overrides.csv through the companion and upload it through the normal Sussex hotload path.
compilerHints.recalibrationNote: Applying this profile resets controller-breathing calibration in the Sussex runtime.
